timeline.label: FY2025 results: revenue $21.9B (+15%); operating income $5.3B; diluted EPS $6.66; $72B 2026-2030 capital plan disclosed
FY2025 dividend declared $3.74/sh; approximately $5.6B equity issuance planned to fund capital plan; $3.5B ATM program available
Management pivots capital plan toward transmission upgrades and new generation to serve data-center and large-load demand across 11-state footprint
Ongoing state rate cases and FERC transmission formula rates govern allowed ROE on growing rate base
Accelerated transmission investment in PJM and ERCOT footprints; Transource joint ventures for regional lines
Coal retirement and renewable additions continue under integrated resource plans across operating companies
AEP remains a publicly traded holding company for vertically integrated and T&D operating utilities in 11 states

industry.structure: Regulated electric utilities operate geographic monopolies with returns set by state commissions and FERC on transmission; AEP is among the largest US wires and generation owners with ~$72B five-year capital plan.
industry.share_shift: Load growth from data centers and industrial electrification favors transmission-heavy utilities with available interconnection capacity; AEP competes for large-load customers with peers DUK, SO, and EXC in overlapping regions.
industry.trend: Affordability pressure on customer bills, rising interest rates on utility debt, and equity issuance to fund capex are the dominant industry themes; data-center demand is the primary growth tailwind for wires owners.
